title: Boeing Vs. L3Harris: Looking At The Dividend Payout Ratio Was A Fool's Errand
text: Relying solely on the payout ratio to determine a company's dividend safety is a misguided approach - there are simply too many moving parts. title: ViaSat-3 Satellite Completes Final Integrated Satellite Test
text: CARLSBAD., Calif. , Nov. 28, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Viasat Inc. (NASDAQ: VSAT), a global leader in satellite communications, announced today the ViaSat-3 Americas satellite has completed its Final Integrated Satellite Test (FIST) and has now progressed to the Flight Final phase of integration where it is undergoing final build-up to its flight configuration.

title: Airbus Faces The Same Challenges As Boeing
text: Airbus lowered its delivery target earlier this year from 720 units to around 700 units. Past performance and current supply chain issues suggest extraordinary performance will be required for Airbus to meet the lowered target.

title: Boeing certification troubles in Canada ground 1st WestJet freighters
text: MIAMI — A delay in getting four 737-800 aircraft, newly converted to freighters by Boeing, certified by the Canadian government has set back WestJet's plans to expand into freighter operations by nine months. The four cargo jets are sitting idle on the tarmac at Calgary International Airport, WestJet's home base, while Boeing awaits approval from aviation authorities for the design changing the used passenger aircraft into a dedicated freighter.

title: 737 MAX crash victim families press U.S. on Boeing settlement
text: Relatives of passengers killed in two Boeing 737 MAX crashes pressed U.S. Justice Department lawyers on Friday to unwind a 2021 deal that allowed the U.S. planemaker to escape criminal prosecution.

title: Boeing Reorganizes After Billions Of Losses
text: Boeing Defense, Space & Security lost $3 billion in the past 12 months. Reorganization of the Defense segment is related to the weak engineering and financial performance that has plagued the company for years.

title: Boeing reorganizes aerospace and defense business, consolidates it in fewer divisions
text: Boeing Co. BA, -0.79% said Thursday it was reorganizing its aerospace and defense unit, going to four divisions from eight, to "accelerate operational discipline, program quality, safety and performance" and put the unit on a "path to stronger, profitable growth." Boeing Global Services will also combine its domestic and international services, the company said.

title: Boeing Replaces Space Chief as Defense Unit Looks to Stem Losses
text: The company said it was replacing the head of its space business as part of a broader restructuring aimed at reversing losses at its defense unit.

title: Silk Way West, Alaska Airlines sign Boeing freighter deals
text: Silk Way West Airlines, a growing cargo operator based in Azerbaijan, and Alaska Airlines on Thursday signed contracts with Boeing for two freighters apiece — but that is where the similarities ended.  Silk Way West said it will take two 777-8s, the largest plane available from Boeing (NYSE: BA) and one so modern it isn't even being produced yet, while Alaska Airlines will use secondhand 737-800s passenger aircraft and turn them into freighters.

title: Boeing says 60% more freighters needed by 2041 to support air cargo

title: Boeing May Not Roll Out A New (Potentially Autonomous) Airliner Until 2035; Promises To Return Cash To Investors In 2026
